faqs.q: What does the vagus nerve do?
faqs.a: It's a long nerve that links the brainstem to organs including the heart, lungs, and much of the digestive tract, and it's a major part of the parasympathetic, rest-and-digest system. It helps slow the heart rate, ease breathing, and support digestion when the body is calm.

faqs.q: Do vagus nerve hacks really calm anxiety?
faqs.a: The vagus nerve isn't a magic off-switch for stress, and a lot of online content overstates what simple hacks can do. The science on these techniques is still developing, so claims should be read with some caution.

faqs.q: Why is the vagus nerve linked to mental health?
faqs.a: Because it's central to the body's calming system, it comes up often in conversations about stress and anxiety. It helps explain why slowing the breath, especially a long exhale, can have a real physical effect on how anxious someone feels.
